rationale: The page provides detailed information on the AMRUT 2.0 mission, including its objectives, components, monitoring mechanisms, and related initiatives. It also includes a section on India's economic ranking as the 4th largest economy according to the IMF.
cleanedText: AMRUT (Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation) • About: Launched in selected 500 cities and towns, focusing on the development of basic infrastructure in the sectors of Water supply, Sewerage and septage management, Storm water drainage, green spaces and parks, and non-motorised urban transport. • AMRUT was subsumed in AMRUT 2.0 in 2021, with the urban transport component excluded from its scope. AMRUT 2.0 • AMRUT 2.0 focuses only on water and sewerage and aims to provide tap water to all households in statutory towns and improve sewerage management in 500 AMRUT cities. • Major Components: o Pey Jal Survekshan: It is a challenge-based assessment in cities to evaluate service level benchmarks for water supply, reuse, sewerage, and water body conservation. o Behaviour Change Communication: To promote water conservation and improve water use efficiency. o Technology Sub-Mission: Promotes start-up ideas and private entrepreneurship through expert-screened pilot projects. AMRUT 2.0 MISSION: Atal Mission for Rejuvenation and Urban Transformation. KEY OBJECTIVES: 1. Universal Water Supply: Coverage in all statutory towns, Ensuring water access for every household. 2. 100% Sewerage Management: In 500 AMRUT cities, Complete septage management systems. 3. Water Secure Cities: Rejuvenation of water bodies, Urban Aquifer Management, Rain water harvesting, PeyJal Survekshan. • Other Features: o Public Private Partnership (PPP) projects: Mandatory in cities with over 1 million people. o Outcome-Based Funding: Cities must submit roadmap to achieve set targets. o Community Participation: with emphasis on women's self-help groups. o Promote circular economy of water: By focusing on recycle/reuse of treated sewage, rejuvenation of water bodies and water conservation. • Monitoring: o National Level: An Apex Committee (AC) chaired by the Secretary, MoHUA. o State Level: State High Powered Steering Committee (SHPSC) chaired by Chief Secretary of State. • Other Key Initiatives Under AMRUT: o Jal Hi AMRIT: To treat and reuse water safely. o AMRUT Mitra: To foster skills among SHG women related to water management in their communities. 5.7. OTHER KEY DEVELOPMENTS. 5.7.1. INDIA 4TH LARGEST ECONOMY. As per the recent World Economic Outlook of the IMF, India has surpassed Japan to become 4th largest economy worldwide. • Top largest economies: US ($30.5 Trillion), China ($19.2 Trillion), Germany ($4.74 Trillion) and India ($4.18 Trillion).
